textByHash.257406985897349bb9f00e1298375b60d395d978: No... It's just... It's been so long! All this time with only myself for company!
textByHash.1983ff7b6c2a821431eebc183d07444c2c249db0: Do you mean the edge of the world? It's been my life's ambition to find it and be the first gnome to stare over its edge.
textByHash.ceadf2f22eb5d14b4821294e953c81893d7f9b7e: Everything has an edge. Eventually I'll find it. I'll keep heading westwards until I get there.
textByHash.e095a6c6deb9b0d8090df68e40df3f7be2c5d91c: Spherical? Don't be silly! The world is clearly flat. If it was ball-shaped, we'd all roll off the side. Silly human.
textByHash.93deec71c493e87e0447a73043a570a594dad91e: Now you're just making fun of me. I don't think you realise the gravity of the situation!
textByHash.94118424107b8a204e4f1b55e3ef54bdd397e947: That is true, but I'm only doing that as a detour from my journey to the edge of the world. I figured it would be on the way.
textByHash.76ef1bbfc529b81e3be218346fa6a40eb5af7319: I think so. There's a waste outlet nearby that I believe may be coming from the city of Arposandra!
textByHash.ce8c523dbe9aa2f4cf22bd9a598770a725208c23: Well, have a look for yourself. There's a huge swamp of nasty goo out west that definitely isn't natural. It's coming from that outlet.
textByHash.e7d2a0ab1af3a0409dc7ea5c056392ef4583f459: Indeed, but there is one other thing. The outlet appears to be patrolled by a creature that suggests gnomish involvement... A giant tortoise!
textByHash.619255a2c0029b92ff4a7a9721ad582a374eb4f8: Exactly! The thing is, there seems to be something wrong with it. It seems... unwell.
textByHash.15303d11f705f06817191e561a7e74faa0a78223: It has some large tentacles growing from underneath its shell.
textByHash.431f016b2372f2afb6310dd980deca0f5dfe67f7: Well, whatever you call it, it's not normal. Maybe it's an illness caused by Glouphrie's magic.
textByHash.ad9434d9043d5def2e04a9860a74175e3e20c6d4: Go for it. The outlet isn't hard to find. It's just to the west.
textByHash.be351713053156a1da10dc96221ebc695a7445e0: It's just to the west of here. Shouldn't be too hard to find.
textByHash.0f32c2c96b66622cf4111155aae570e04ff6c4d7: Oh yes, I forgot about that. Poor thing isn't looking great, but I'm not sure what can be done. Trees aren't really my area of expertise.
textByHash.a626b7f32fc267319aa518c2730c4eb63b8fa68d: Hello there. Any luck with that outlet?
textByHash.f27f5b9ccadf0a4324b94145d51750ac7da7a792: Hello there. How goes the hunt for Arposandra?
textByHash.b251c9461950dc419ae1604d2ddc03ca011cf837: Talking terrorbirds? There must be some strange magic being practised in there. What do you plan to do?
textByHash.8d99c6069894d8aab25a5d8c462341eb2b15ba30: That's a shame. Still, you've made a big discovery. It will be quite the tale to tell... assuming anyone believes a story about talking terrorbirds, that is.
textByHash.ce6bdeed7ac93e11ed5790f0963e8ec462af5fa7: Truth be told, I've found myself getting a bit side- tracked. The potential discovery of Arposandra is quite an exciting one!
textByHash.395d361c7c36c49c9f470d8f158c632946ef1952: Yes, that's why I've not dared go inside again. It's much safer out here. Slightly less smelly as well.
textByHash.3294cc22ade18256f3ac3dd887e9568830977276: That wasn't very clever, was it?
textByHash.2614a79cd3f168361f6bb504e181d718facc8853: Well, it's a good job explorers like me know how to use their surroundings. I have an idea!
